anti_patterns: Calling analyze-architecture (Opus) for a one-line fix — Token Economy violation
Skipping review-code on medium tasks — false economy, review catches bugs cheaper than production
Running full pipeline on config changes — overhead without return
Never using review-security — security debt accumulates silently
Hardcoding profile names in pipelines — pipelines must reference functions, Leo resolves profiles at delegation time
content.context: Leo orchestrates development tasks by delegating to Specialists (Sonnet-powered ephemeral sub-agents). Each Specialist has a profile that defines their discipline. The question is: how many and which Specialists should Leo involve for a given task?

examples.task: Add input validation to login form
Refactor auth middleware to support OAuth

content.problem: Using the full pipeline (analyze-architecture → execute → write-tests → review-code → review-security) for every task wastes tokens and adds latency. Using only one function for complex tasks risks quality. Leo needs a sizing heuristic.

solution.description: Leo sizes each task before delegating and selects the appropriate pipeline. Pipelines are PROJECT-AGNOSTIC — they reference abstract functions (roles), not concrete profiles. Leo resolves each function to a concrete profile at delegation time based on project context.

pipeline_tiers.rationale: One function, no overhead. Self-QA in the resolved profile covers quality.
pipeline_tiers.triggers: Bug fix, config change, typo, one-file edit

pipeline_tiers.rationale: Execution plus adversarial review. Most tasks land here.
analyze-architecture (Opus) evaluates approach before burning execution tokens. write-tests ensures coverage. review-code closes.
pipeline_tiers.triggers: New feature, architecture change, new subsystem

pipeline_tiers.pipeline: Add review-security before delivery to ANY pipeline above
pipeline_tiers.rationale: This is additive, not a separate tier. Any task touching security-sensitive surface gets a review-security pass regardless of size.
pipeline_tiers.triggers: Auth, payments, personal data, secrets, API keys

profile_resolution.description: Leo resolves abstract pipeline functions to concrete Specialist profiles based on the project's stack and context. The same pipeline pattern works identically across all projects — only the resolution changes.

examples.resolves_to: Code Reviewer profile (usually generic, but could be specialized per stack)

sizing_heuristic: How many files will this touch? (1 = small, 2-5 = medium, 5+ = large)
Does this change architecture or introduce new patterns? (yes = large)
Does this touch auth, payments, secrets, or personal data? (yes = add security review)
Is this a known pattern or a novel problem? (novel = bump up one tier)
